steps.title: Convert decay to a multiplier
steps.content: A decrease of $18\%$ means $82\%$ of the mass remains after each period, so the decay multiplier is $0.82$.

steps.title: Count the time periods
steps.content:
One decay period is $9.09$ minutes. After $t$ minutes, the number of periods is
$$
\frac{t}{9.09}
$$

steps.title: Build the model
steps.content: Using an initial mass of $100$, the model is $$ M=100(0.82)^{\frac{t}{9.09}} $$ The answer is D.
